PCF: Page Configuration File — Guidewire's XML-based UI framework used to define screens, widgets, and layouts in PolicyCenter, ClaimCenter, and BillingCenter.
Gosu: Guidewire's proprietary scripting language, based on Java syntax. Used to write business logic, rules, and customizations within Guidewire applications. Every Gosu customization is a potential upgrade liability.
PolicyCenter: Guidewire's Policy Administration System (PAS). Manages the full policy lifecycle including quotes, new business, endorsements, renewals, and cancellations.
ClaimCenter: Guidewire's Claims Management System. Handles the end-to-end claims lifecycle from FNOL through settlement and closure.
BillingCenter: Guidewire's Billing Management System. Manages premium billing, payment plans, disbursements, and agency billing.
InsuranceSuite: Guidewire's integrated suite of core insurance systems — PolicyCenter, ClaimCenter, and BillingCenter — deployed together on a shared platform.
GWCP: Guidewire Cloud Platform — Guidewire's SaaS offering that hosts InsuranceSuite in a managed cloud environment, replacing on-premises deployments.
FNOL: First Notice of Loss — the initial report made to an insurance company describing a loss or damage event. Triggers the claims process in ClaimCenter.
SmartCOMM: Smart Communications' document composition platform. Used in Guidewire implementations for generating policy documents, EOBs, and customer correspondence.
Exstream: OpenText's enterprise document composition platform used for generating insurance policy documents and communications. Functionally similar to SmartCOMM.
EIS: EIS Group's insurance core platform (PolicyCore, BillingCore, ClaimCore, CustomerCore) — a competing core system to Guidewire used by some P&C carriers.
Duck Creek: A P&C insurance platform provider offering Policy, Billing, and Claims systems. A key competitor to Guidewire in the core systems market.
Majesco: A cloud-native insurance platform provider, competing with Guidewire and Duck Creek in the P&C core systems space.
MuleSoft: Salesforce's integration platform (Anypoint Platform). Used to build APIs and integration flows connecting Guidewire to external systems like payment gateways, rating services, and portals.
SAFe: Scaled Agile Framework — an enterprise-scale Agile methodology used for coordinating large delivery teams across multiple workstreams with Program Increment (PI) Planning cadences.
PI Planning: Program Increment Planning — a SAFe ceremony where all teams align on a common mission and identify cross-team dependencies for the upcoming 10-week increment.
SCRUM: An Agile framework using short iterative sprints (typically 2 weeks) with daily standups, sprint planning, sprint review, and retrospectives.
RAG: Retrieval Augmented Generation — a GenAI pattern that grounds LLM responses in a specific document corpus. Used in claims and underwriting automation to process insurance documents intelligently.
LangChain: An open-source framework for building LLM-powered applications. Used to chain together prompts, retrievers, and tools to build RAG pipelines and AI agents.
SLA: Service Level Agreement — contractually defined performance commitments covering response times, resolution times, and uptime targets, typically for Application Managed Services (AMS).
AMS: Application Managed Services — post-go-live support and operations for Guidewire and related systems, including incident management, upgrades, monitoring, and enhancements.
SOW: Statement of Work — the contractual document defining project scope, deliverables, timeline, and commercial terms for a professional services engagement.
RACI: Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed — a matrix used in project management to clarify roles and ownership for tasks and decisions across teams.
RFC: Request for Change — a formal document initiating a change to project scope. Typically triggers a change order and commercial adjustment.
P&C: Property and Casualty — the segment of insurance covering damage to property (homeowners, commercial property) and liability for accidents (auto, workers comp, general liability).
WC: Workers Compensation — a line of P&C insurance providing wage replacement and medical benefits to employees injured on the job. CompSource Mutual is an example mono-line WC carrier.
LOB: Line of Business — a specific category of insurance product, such as personal auto, homeowners, commercial property, or workers compensation.
DOI: Department of Insurance — the state regulatory body overseeing insurance companies, products, and rates. Carriers must file rate changes and new products with the DOI for approval.
P&L: Profit and Loss — the financial measure of a business unit or engagement, calculated as revenue minus all associated costs. AVP-level leaders own P&L accountability.
CI/CD: Continuous Integration / Continuous Deployment — automated pipelines that build, test, and deploy code changes frequently and reliably, reducing deployment time from hours to minutes.
DevOps: A set of practices combining software development and IT operations, emphasizing automation, CI/CD pipelines, and continuous delivery to shorten release cycles.
ETL: Extract, Transform, Load — the process of pulling data from source systems, transforming it to a target schema, and loading it into a destination like Snowflake or a data warehouse.
Terraform: HashiCorp's Infrastructure as Code (IaC) tool. Used to provision and manage cloud resources (AWS, Azure, GCP) declaratively through configuration files.
Snowflake: A cloud-native data warehouse platform. Used in insurance analytics for loss ratio analysis, regulatory reporting, and self-service BI dashboards.
IaC: Infrastructure as Code — managing and provisioning cloud infrastructure through machine-readable configuration files (Terraform, CloudFormation) rather than manual processes.
API: Application Programming Interface — a defined contract for how two software systems communicate, typically over HTTP using REST or SOAP protocols.
AWS: Amazon Web Services — Amazon's cloud computing platform. Key services used in insurance delivery: EC2, S3, Lambda, Glue, Kinesis, and API Gateway.
Azure: Microsoft Azure — Microsoft's cloud computing platform. Provides Azure OpenAI, DevOps tooling, and infrastructure services used in Guidewire cloud integrations.
GCP: Google Cloud Platform — Google's suite of cloud computing services, including compute, storage, AI, and data analytics products.
Kubernetes: An open-source container orchestration platform for automating deployment, scaling, and management of containerized applications.
Docker: A platform for packaging applications and their dependencies into portable containers that run consistently across development, staging, and production environments.
NPS: Net Promoter Score — a customer loyalty metric measured by asking how likely customers are to recommend a product or service, scored from -100 to +100.
RACI: Responsible, Accountable, Consulted, Informed — a project management matrix clarifying roles and ownership for tasks and decisions.
PartnerConnect: Guidewire's partner program with tiers (Select, Advantage, Premier) for consulting and technology firms. Higher tiers reflect deeper certified expertise and transaction volume.
dbt: Data Build Tool — an open-source framework for transforming data in cloud warehouses using SQL. Used with Snowflake for insurance analytics and reporting.
Kafka: Apache Kafka — a distributed event streaming platform used for real-time data pipelines and event-driven architectures between Guidewire and downstream systems.
